title: Jev Sunrider
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}}

|death=Circa [[4008 BBY]]<ref name="4008 BBY">Per [[Star Wars Omnibus: Tales of the Jedi Volume 1|''Star Wars Omnibus: Tales of the Jedi, Volume 1'']], [[Andur Sunrider]] left the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[H'ratth]] for the world of [[Ambria/Legends|Ambria]] in around [[3999 BBY]]. The sourcebook ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]'' states that Andur spent two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] on H'ratth before leaving for Ambria and that six years prior to his arrival on H'ratth he saw the [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] of his grandfather Jev. As the ''Companion'' also states that Jev disappeared on a mission a year prior to his appearance to Andur as a [[The Force/Legends|Force]] ghost, Jev's death can therefore be dated to around [[4008 BBY]].</ref>
|species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="TOTJC">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''</ref>

{{Quote|It is up to you. You will play a part in the coming war. But do not resist the role the Force wishes you to play, for things are not always what they seem.|Jev Sunrider, to his grandson Andur|<ref name="TOTJC" />}}
'''Jev Sunrider''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Watchman]] of the [[Darada system/Legends|Darada system]] in the [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] leading up to the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Around the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[4008 BBY]], Sunrider left his [[Sunrider family|family's]] home on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Darada]] and, along with three other [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Masters, set out for the nearby [[Star system/Legends|system]] of [[Krayiss II|Krayiss Two]]; this mission would ultimately end in the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of all four Jedi at the hands of a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] force. His [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] later appeared to his grandson, [[Andur Sunrider|Andur]], in which Jev told Andur that he would play an important role in a coming war.

==Biography==
===Jedi Watchman===
Jev Sunrider was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Old Sith Wars]]. During his career as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Sunrider attained the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] and became close friends with his fellow Jedi Master [[Chamma]]. Sunrider was also appointed as the [[Jedi Watchman/Legends|Watchman]] of the [[Darada system/Legends|Darada system]] and established his [[Sunrider family|family's]] home within the [[Star system/Legends|system]], on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Darada]]. There, Jev was present for much of the [[Youngling/Legends|childhood]] of his grandson, the young [[Andur Sunrider]]. Throughout Andur's early life, Jev taught his grandson much about the history and lore of the Jedi Order, as well as the ever-constant war the Jedi were engaged in against the followers of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Due to his grandfather's teachings, Andur made the decision early on that he wanted to follow in Jev's footsteps, and walk the path of a Jedi when he grew older.<ref name="TOTJC" />

Around the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[4008 BBY]],<ref name="4008 BBY" /> the [[Standard day/Legends|day]] before Andur's twelfth [[Birthday/Legends|birthday]], Jev and three other Jedi Masters departed from Darada and set course for the nearby [[Star/Legends|star]] system of [[Krayiss II|Krayiss Two]]. There, they discovered a growing dark side force, and the mission ultimately resulted in the [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of all four Jedi. Following their departure, [[Standard month/Legends|months]] passed with no word from Jev or any of his companions; this caused young Andur to fall into a deep depression, fearing that he would never see his grandfather again. Nevertheless, approximately one year after he had left for Krayiss Two, Jev's [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] appeared to Andur as he was working in his [[Unidentified Sunrider male|father's]] [[Garden/Legends|garden]]. At first, Andur was scared by the specter before him, but Jev's ghost told him to calm himself, and allow any fear to slip away from his mind. At that moment, Jev advised Andur that he would have an important part to play in a [[Great Sith War/Legends|coming war]], and that he should not resist the role which [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] had laid out for him. However, before Andur could respond to the spirit of his grandfather, Jev's ghost dissipated, leaving his grandson to ponder on his words.<ref name="TOTJC" />

===Legacy===
Following the death of his grandfather, Andur completely devoted himself to learning the ways of the Force and was eventually invited by Jev's old friend Chamma to study under him on the planet of [[H'ratth]]. Years later, Jev's prophecy to his grandson came true when Andur was [[Confrontation on the Stenness Hyperspace Terminal|killed]] by criminals in the employ of [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]. As his grandfather had done for him, Andur reappeared as a Force ghost to his wife, [[Nomi Sunrider|Nomi]], and encouraged her to pick up his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and defend herself and their child, [[Vima Sunrider|Vima]].<ref name="TOTJC" /> Due to Andur reappearing as a Force ghost to his wife, Nomi was propelled down the road of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi knighthood]] and would play a leading role in helping to defeat the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] forces during the Great Sith War.<ref name="TSW6">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 6|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 6]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Jev Sunrider was well versed in the history of the Jedi Order and taught much of his knowledge to his young grandson, Andur. Likewise, due to his being a Jedi Watchman, Jev was knowledgeable of the constant war the Jedi were involved in against those who followed the dark side of the Force; because of this Jev continued Andur's teachings by constantly reminding the young boy of the fights between the forces of light and dark. Due to numerous teachings and influences of his grandfather, Andur made the decision as a young man to follow in his grandfather's steps and become a Jedi.<ref name="TOTJC" />

==Powers and abilities==
Following his death on Krayiss Two, Jev would use the Force to reappear to his grandson, Andur, as a ghost to tell him that he would play a crucial role in the coming war.<ref name="TOTJC" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Jev Sunrider was developed by author [[George R. Strayton]] for the [[1996]] [[West End Games]] [[roleplaying game]] source book ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''. The only mention of Jev in the source book consisted of a few sentences incorporated into the introduction Andur Sunrider's entry; there, Jev's story helped establish the backstory for his grandson.<ref name="TOTJC" />

Though ''Tales of the Jedi Companion'' included references to Andur Sunrider's father, who resided at the Sunrider family home on Darada, it did not explicitly confirm that Jev Sunrider was the father to this individual.<ref name="TOTJC" />

==Sources==
